author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
nitāi⋅gaura⋅nāma, ānandera dhāma,
jei jana nāhi laya
tā’re jamarāya, dha’re la’ye jāya,
narake ḍubāya tāya

tulasīra hāra, nā pare je chāra,
jamālaye vāsa tā’ra
tilaka dhāraṇa, nā kare je⋅jana,
vṛthāya janama tā’ra

nā laya hari-nāma, vidhi tā’re bāma,
pāmara pāṣaṇḍamati
vaiṣṇava⋅sevana, nā kare je⋅jana,
ki ha’be tāhāra gati

gurumantra sāra, kara eibāra,
vrajete hoibe vāsa
tamo-guṇa jā’be, sattva⋅guṇa pā’be,
hoibe kṛṣṇera dāsa

e dāsa locana, bale anukṣaṇa,
(nitāi) gauraguṇa gāo sukhe
ei rase jā’ra, rati nā hoila,
cūṇa⋅kāli tā’ra mukhe
en_translation:
The names of Nitāi and Gaura are the abode of bliss. Whoever does not chant these names will be seized by the lord of death and drowned in hell.

That wretched person who does not wear a Tulasi garland will dwell in the abode of death. Whoever does not wear a sacred tilaka, his birth is in vain.

Whoever does not chant the Holy Name is sinful and stone-hearted, and fate is against him. Whoever does not render service to the devotees, what will be his destination?

Take the essence of the guru's mantra now, and you will then reside in Vraja. The mode of ignorance will depart, you will attain the mode of goodness, and you will become a servant of Kṛṣṇa.

This servant Locana constantly says: "Happily sing the glories of Gaura." Whoever does not develop affection for this nectar—disgrace be upon his face!
